description_long: This object is a small potted plant with a dense cluster of narrow, silvery-green leaves that resemble those of an Artemisia or lavender species. The foliage is bushy and appears soft, with a slightly frosted or dusty appearance, giving it a muted, almost grayish tone. The plant is supported by a short, thick, brown stem that emerges from the base, which is not visibly contained within a traditional pot, suggesting a minimalist or root-ball style presentation. The leaves are finely textured and radiate outward in all directions, creating a rounded, full canopy. There are no visible flowers or buds, and the plant's overall form is compact and ornamental. The absence of a visible pot or decorative elements emphasizes the natural beauty of the foliage.
description: A compact, bushy potted plant with silvery-green, narrow leaves and a thick brown stem, presented without a visible decorative pot. The foliage is dense and soft-looking, forming a rounded canopy.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dense, rounded foliage and thick central stem, with leaves radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the bushy spread of the leaves and the slight angle of the stem, with foliage extending horizontally.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the plant, showing the full volume of the leaf cluster and the stem's base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the underside of the foliage and the curvature of the stem, emphasizing the plant's compactness.
